questions.stem: Under ASC 842, which of the following criteria would classify a lease as a finance lease for the lessee?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The lease term is for 50% of the remaining economic life of the asset

choices.label: B
choices.text: The present value of lease payments equals 85% of the fair value of the asset

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ownership of the asset transfers to the lessee at the end of the lease term

choices.label: D
choices.text: The lease contains a renewal option

questions.explanation: ASC 842-10-25-2 provides five criteria for finance lease classification. Transfer of ownership at the end of the lease term is one of them. The other criteria include: a purchase option reasonably certain to be exercised, lease term is for the major part (typically 75%) of the remaining economic life, present value of payments amounts to substantially all (typically 90%) of the fair value, and the asset is specialized with no alternative use.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-25-2はファイナンスリースの分類に関する5つの基準を規定しています。リース期間終了時の所有権移転はその1つです。その他の基準には、行使されることが合理的に確実な購入オプション、リース期間が残存経済的耐用年数の大部分（通常75%）であること、支払いの現在価値が公正価値のほぼ全て（通常90%）に相当すること、資産が特殊で代替的用途がないことが含まれます。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Lessee Corp enters into a 5-year operating lease with annual payments of $10,000 due at the end of each year. The lessee's incremental borrowing rate is 6%. The present value of the lease payments is $42,124. What is the initial measurement of the right-of-use asset?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $42,124 plus any initial direct costs and prepaid lease payments

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-30-5, the right-of-use asset is initially measured at the amount of the lease liability ($42,124), plus any lease payments made at or before commencement, plus any initial direct costs incurred by the lessee, minus any lease incentives received.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-30-5に基づき、使用権資産はリース負債の金額（$42,124）に、開始日以前に支払われたリース料、借手が負担した当初直接コストを加算し、受領したリースインセンティブを控除した金額で当初測定されます。

questions.stem: Under ASC 842, how is the lease expense for an operating lease recognized by the lessee?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Interest expense on the lease liability and amortization of the right-of-use asset separately

choices.label: B
choices.text: A single lease cost on a straight-line basis over the lease term

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only when lease payments are made

choices.label: D
choices.text: Front-loaded, with higher expense in earlier periods

questions.explanation: ASC 842-20-25-6 states that for an operating lease, a lessee shall recognize a single lease cost, calculated so that the cost is allocated over the lease term on a generally straight-line basis. This differs from a finance lease, where amortization and interest are recognized separately, resulting in front-loaded expense.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-25-6は、オペレーティングリースについて、借手はリース期間にわたり概ね定額で配分されるように計算された単一のリース費用を認識すると規定しています。これは、償却費と利息が別々に認識され、費用が前倒しになるファイナンスリースとは異なります。

questions.stem: Lessee has an existing operating lease with 3 years remaining and a carrying value of the right-of-use asset of $30,000 and lease liability of $32,000. The lease is modified to extend the term by 2 years with no other changes. The modification does not grant an additional right of use. How should the lessee account for this modification?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a new separate lease

choices.label: B
choices.text: Remeasure the lease liability and adjust the right-of-use asset

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize a gain or loss for the difference

choices.label: D
choices.text: Continue to account for the original lease without adjustment

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-25-8, if a lease modification does not result in a separate lease, the lessee remeasures the lease liability at the modification date using a revised discount rate, and adjusts the right-of-use asset by the same amount as the change in the lease liability.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-25-8に基づき、リースの変更が独立したリースとならない場合、借手は変更日に改訂された割引率を使用してリース負債を再測定し、リース負債の変動額と同額で使用権資産を調整します。

questions.stem: Under ASC 842, what is the maximum lease term (at commencement) for a lessee to elect the short-term lease exemption?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 6 months

choices.label: B
choices.text: 12 months

choices.label: C
choices.text: 12 months and the lease does not include a purchase option that the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise

choices.label: D
choices.text: 24 months

questions.explanation: ASC 842-20-25-2 permits a lessee to elect not to apply the recognition requirements for short-term leases. A short-term lease is defined as a lease that, at the commencement date, has a lease term of 12 months or less and does not include an option to purchase the underlying asset that the lessee is reasonably certain to exercise.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-25-2は、短期リースについて認識要件を適用しないことを借手が選択できると規定しています。短期リースとは、開始日において、リース期間が12か月以下であり、かつ借手が行使することが合理的に確実な原資産の購入オプションを含まないリースと定義されています。

questions.stem: Entity F sells a building to Entity G for its fair value of $1,000,000 (carrying value $700,000) and simultaneously leases it back. The leaseback is classified as an operating lease and does not transfer control back to Entity F. What gain should Entity F recognize at the transaction date?

choices.label: C
choices.text: The portion of the gain related to the rights transferred to Entity G

choices.label: D
choices.text: $300,000 amortized over the lease term

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-40-25-3, if the transfer of the asset qualifies as a sale, the seller-lessee recognizes only the amount of any gain or loss that relates to the rights transferred to the buyer-lessor. The portion relating to the right-of-use asset retained through the leaseback is not recognized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-40-25-3に基づき、資産の移転が売却として適格である場合、売手兼借手は買手兼貸手に移転した権利に関連する利益または損失の金額のみを認識します。リースバックを通じて保持する使用権資産に関連する部分は認識されません。

questions.stem: A lease agreement requires the lessee to pay $5,000 per month plus 2% of monthly sales exceeding $100,000. Under ASC 842, how should the variable payments based on sales be treated in measuring the lease liability?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Included in the lease liability using an estimate of future sales

choices.label: B
choices.text: Excluded from the lease liability and recognized in the period incurred

choices.label: C
choices.text: Included using the expected value method

choices.label: D
choices.text: Capitalized separately from the lease liability

questions.explanation: ASC 842-10-30-5 states that variable lease payments that depend on an index or rate are included in the lease liability, but variable payments based on performance or usage (such as sales-based payments) are excluded from the measurement of the lease liability and recognized in profit or loss in the period in which the obligation is incurred.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-30-5は、指数またはレートに連動する変動リース料はリース負債に含まれるが、業績または使用量に基づく変動支払い（売上ベースの支払いなど）はリース負債の測定から除外され、義務が発生した期間に損益で認識されると規定しています。

questions.stem: Under ASC 842, a lessor classifies a lease as a sales-type lease. At commencement, the fair value of the asset is $500,000, the carrying amount is $400,000, and the present value of lease payments is $500,000. What is the lessor's gross profit at commencement?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-30-25-1, at commencement of a sales-type lease, the lessor derecognizes the underlying asset, recognizes a net investment in the lease, and recognizes any selling profit or loss. The gross profit equals the fair value (or PV of lease payments if lower) minus the carrying amount: $500,000 - $400,000 = $100,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-30-25-1に基づき、販売型リースの開始時に、貸手は原資産の認識を中止し、リースに対する正味投資を認識し、売却損益を認識します。粗利益は公正価値（またはリース料の現在価値が低い場合はその額）から帳簿価額を差し引いた額です：$500,000 - $400,000 = $100,000。

questions.stem: When determining the present value of lease payments, what discount rate should a lessee use if the rate implicit in the lease is not readily determinable?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The risk-free rate

choices.label: B
choices.text: The lessee's incremental borrowing rate

choices.label: C
choices.text: The prime rate

choices.label: D
choices.text: The lessor's implicit rate

questions.explanation: ASC 842-20-30-3 states that a lessee should use the rate implicit in the lease unless that rate is not readily determinable, in which case the lessee uses its incremental borrowing rate. The incremental borrowing rate is the rate the lessee would have to pay to borrow on a collateralized basis over a similar term.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-30-3は、借手はリースの計算利子率を使用すべきであるが、その利率が容易に算定できない場合は追加借入利子率を使用すると規定しています。追加借入利子率とは、借手が類似の期間にわたり担保付で借入を行う場合に支払わなければならない利率です。

questions.stem: A lessee enters a finance lease with an initial right-of-use asset of $100,000 and a lease term of 10 years. The asset has no residual value. Using straight-line amortization, what is the annual amortization expense for the right-of-use asset?

choices.label: D
choices.text: It varies each year based on the effective interest method

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-35-7, for a finance lease, the right-of-use asset is amortized on a straight-line basis (unless another systematic basis is more representative) from the commencement date to the earlier of the end of the useful life or the end of the lease term. $100,000 / 10 years = $10,000 per year.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-35-7に基づき、ファイナンスリースの使用権資産は、開始日から耐用年数の終了またはリース期間の終了のいずれか早い方まで、定額法（他の規則的な基準がより適切でない限り）で償却されます。$100,000 / 10年 = 年間$10,000。
